angle: BM NOCTURNAL BLUE POSTER. Midnight cobalt #14213D ground DOMINANT 80% with 10% tonal gradient (darker toward corners). Velvet burgundy #4A1621 deep-gradient sub-field at ONE corner only, <15% of canvas. TOP 30%: masthead in METALLIC BLUE FOIL gradient serif caps (cobalt #1E3A8A → deep sapphire → cobalt with specular) Farnham/Canela Italic 180-230px stacked 2 lines tight tracking, hairline warm-tan rule beneath. MIDDLE 40%: single hand-drawn warm-tan or metallic-blue COPITA silhouette centered, line-art only, hairline underline, 3 small smoke curls. LOWER 25%: small-caps warm-tan support info-lockup at 30-36px tracked wide, hairline tan rule separators. BOTTOM strip: tiny warm-cream dateline small-caps wide-tracked. NO brass, NO gold, NO black-dominant ground. Blue Mezcal nocturnal = DEEP BLUE with burgundy + tan accents. 5-7% film grain.
subject: Mezcal Monday.
prompt:
A social media image for Blue Mezcal, nocturnal after-dark register — insider mezcal bar at 11pm. Reference anchors: Casa Azul NYC after-hours, Leyenda, Ghost Donkey — BUT rendered in Blue Mezcal's OWN palette. Deep-blue editorial restraint, NOT nightclub neon, NOT black+gold steakhouse. Blue Mezcal nocturnal stays BLUE.

PRIMARY GROUND: deep midnight cobalt #14213D transitioning to near-black cobalt #0A1328 as a subtle tonal gradient (5–10% shift, darker toward one corner) — NEVER a flat fill. Deep blue shadow falloff. Charred black #0B0B0B may be a minor corner accent only (<10%); it is NOT the primary ground. The post must read BLUE at first glance, not black.

PRIMARY TYPE TREATMENT: METALLIC BLUE GRADIENT FOIL on headlines — bright cobalt #1E3A8A → deep sapphire #0E2A7A → bright cobalt #1E3A8A as a soft 3-stop gradient reading like embossed foil on cocktail-menu cardstock. Add a specular highlight and a 1px deeper-shade inner edge so type reads as actual metallic foil, not flat blue. Secondary type in warm tan #E8DBB8 or warm cream #F2EADF.

BRAND PALETTE (nocturnal): midnight cobalt #14213D ground dominant. Metallic blue foil #1E3A8A for hero type. Velvet burgundy #4A1621 used as a DEEP TONAL GRADIENT SUB-FIELD at one corner only (darker toward the corner, fading to near-ground) — burgundy is an ACCENT, never dominant, cap at 15%. Warm tan #E8DBB8 for body type and hairline rules. Bone cream #F3ECD8 for supporting accents. NO brass foil. NO metallic gold. NO black-dominant grounds. The nocturnal register = DEEP BLUE with burgundy + tan accents.

TYPOGRAPHY: refined editorial serif italic (Bodoni / Canela / Didot feel) rendered AS metallic blue foil for hero, paired with small-caps grotesque labels in warm tan or warm cream. Hairline blue or tan rules separating sections. Tight tracking on italics.

TEXTURE: fine noise grain 5–8% overlay on every field; subtle paper-grain impression UNDER the metallic-blue-foil type (as if foil stamped on textured menu cardstock); 35mm Portra 800 film-grain emulation on all photography; faint edge vignette pulling focus inward.

Photography style: moody low-light, tungsten 2700K, single rim light from upper-right at 45°, deep blues, the drink/dish glows while the surroundings fall away into cobalt shadow. 40–50% fill band — confident restraint.

FORMAT: Event announcement poster (menu launch, live music, pop-up, collab). Poster-aesthetic typographic stack.

COMPOSITION: symmetric menu-card layout OR photo-bed with 30% black overlay and horizontal date band across middle

HEADLINE OPTIONS (pick ONE — variation-dependent — see COPY PERSONALITY RULE in stem):
  (a) DEFAULT — subject name verbatim, no paraphrasing, no misspellings: "Mezcal Monday."
  (b) a feeling/mood line derived FROM THIS SPECIFIC DISH (use the body-copy phrase below as raw material — e.g. if the phrase is "Black salt rim. Cucumber crown. Cool and dangerous." a valid mood line is "Cool and dangerous.").
  (c) a personality line DERIVED FROM the specific dish + the brand's voice signature (see VOICE SEED in the brand stem). Do NOT reuse example lines from OTHER brands' voice seeds — generate fresh copy that ONLY makes sense for THIS dish at THIS restaurant.
  Default heavily to (a). Use (b) or (c) only when the line adds real personality and still identifies the dish. If using (b)/(c), subject name may appear as small subhead or be omitted.
BODY COPY (quote exactly when rendered): "Half-off flights · 5–8pm · Espadín · Tobalá · Cuishe"
BOTTOM-MARGIN LINE — render this EXACT text at the bottom margin as a single low-key hairline-ruled line: "826 Kohl Ave · Middletown, DE — Visit us · link in bio"
NO-LITERAL-LABEL RULE (non-negotiable): NEVER print any of these meta-label words on the final image — they are instructions to you, NOT content to render: CTA, ADDRESS, SUPPORT LINE, FOOTER LINE, BOTTOM-MARGIN LINE, HEADLINE, SUBHEAD, BODY COPY, HEADER, LABEL, COPY OPTION, VARIATION, WORDMARK, AWARD BADGE, WORD-SPILL, DEFAULT. If any of those words leak onto the canvas, the image fails review.
NO LOGO ATTACHED: skip the brand-mark entirely. DO NOT invent or generate a logo.
Small text-only wordmark acceptable at 40–80px, 50–70% opacity: "BLUE MEZCAL"

VARIATION DIRECTION: BM NOCTURNAL BLUE POSTER. Midnight cobalt #14213D ground DOMINANT 80% with 10% tonal gradient (darker toward corners). Velvet burgundy #4A1621 deep-gradient sub-field at ONE corner only, <15% of canvas. TOP 30%: masthead in METALLIC BLUE FOIL gradient serif caps (cobalt #1E3A8A → deep sapphire → cobalt with specular) Farnham/Canela Italic 180-230px stacked 2 lines tight tracking, hairline warm-tan rule beneath. MIDDLE 40%: single hand-drawn warm-tan or metallic-blue COPITA silhouette centered, line-art only, hairline underline, 3 small smoke curls. LOWER 25%: small-caps warm-tan support info-lockup at 30-36px tracked wide, hairline tan rule separators. BOTTOM strip: tiny warm-cream dateline small-caps wide-tracked. NO brass, NO gold, NO black-dominant ground. Blue Mezcal nocturnal = DEEP BLUE with burgundy + tan accents. 5-7% film grain.

PHOTO RULE: No photograph is needed for this format — build the composition entirely from typography, colour fields, motifs, and textures.

CONTRAST GUARD (non-negotiable, applies to EVERY text-over-anything decision):
  Before finalizing the layout, estimate the luminance of the type and the luminance of
  whatever sits behind it (ground field OR the pixels of the photo under the type).
  If both are dark (type luma <30%% AND ground luma <40%%) OR both are light (type luma
  >70%% AND ground luma >60%%), REJECT and re-choose. Type must read at arm's length on
  a phone — if legibility is marginal, change the type hex to the opposite luminance pole
  of the brand palette. No dark-on-dark, no light-on-light, ever.

TEXT-FIT-CONTAINER RULE (non-negotiable):
  Type SIZES TO THE CONTAINER, not the other way around. If the headline doesn't fit at
  a legible size in the designated color-block / strip / wedge, choose ONE of: (i) shrink
  the type to legibility-min only IF it still reads at arm's length, (ii) expand the
  container to match the type, (iii) break the headline across 2–3 lines with generous
  leading. NEVER cram letters, NEVER rotate 90° to fit a narrow strip, NEVER letter-clip
  at the canvas edge. If a chosen variation angle forces any of these, pick a better
  container geometry.

NO-HERO-OCCLUSION RULE:
  Diagonal wedges, horizontal bands across the middle third, and any geometric shape that
  CUTS THROUGH the primary photo region are forbidden. Color fields sit adjacent to the
  photo, extend from one edge inward, or occupy a corner — they never bisect the dish or
  glass. If the chosen variation angle creates a slash through the hero, reshape it.

FACTUAL-INTEGRITY RULE (NON-NEGOTIABLE — HIGHEST PRIORITY):
  This is real restaurant marketing for real clients. Every word rendered on the image
  MUST be factually accurate. DO NOT INVENT:
    - Staff names, titles, or roles (a person named on a post must actually work there)
    - Magazine / publication / quarterly titles ('Blue Mezcal Quarterly' does not exist)
    - Issue numbers, edition labels, 'Vol. II' references
    - Event names not supplied in this prompt
    - Drink names, dish names, prices, dates, times not supplied
    - Award names, certifications, rankings
    - 'Est. [year]' dates unless supplied
    - Fake product lines, merchandise, collaborations
  ONLY use factual content that comes from these exact sources:
    1. The 'subject' field supplied in this prompt
    2. The 'support' field supplied in this prompt
    3. The brand profile's documented address, wordmark, and operational lines
    4. Documented voice seeds (dichos, signatures) written in the brand stem
  If a layout feels empty without more copy, REDUCE THE COPY rather than invent — negative
  space is always better than fabricated facts.

CROSS-BRAND ISOLATION (non-negotiable):
  Staff, bartender, and chef names are brand-specific and NEVER cross between brands.
  - Selena = Jackson House bartender ONLY. Never render 'Selena' on a Blue Mezcal or
    Azteca post under any circumstance.
  - Do not invent team-member names for any brand. If no real staff name is supplied,
    skip the personality-credit line entirely.

LUXURY-TEXTURE RULE (non-negotiable):
  * Every colour field MUST have a subtle tonal gradient (5–12% shift from one edge to     the opposite edge). NEVER use a pure flat hex fill. Flat fills read cheap and digital.
  * Every colour field MUST carry a texture overlay: 3–8% noise/grain is the minimum.     Add one of: cream laid paper, linen weave, letterpress emboss, brass foil, film     grain, halftone dots, or risograph misregistration — whichever matches the register.
  * Every typographic element MUST carry texture too: letterpress emboss for Jackson     House (type sits AS IF pressed into cream paper, 2px inner-shadow dark edge + 1px     light highlight); brass foil with subtle specular for Blue Mezcal nocturnal; ink-    bleed / halftone offset for Azteca playful; paper-grain impression for Blue Mezcal     editorial.
  * Photography receives Portra 400 or Portra 800 film-grain emulation (depending on     lighting register) — never a clean digital file.
  * Where a drop shadow is used, follow the pro spec: y-offset 4–8px, blur 16–40px,     opacity 8–20%, colour = a DARKER SHADE of the background (never pure black).     NEVER the default Photoshop 5px-90°-75%-black drop shadow.

LUXURY-GRADIENT EXAMPLES (pick one that fits):
  * Paper-gradient: cream lightens toward the centre, darkens at the edges (letterpress     vignette).
  * Tonal-brand-gradient: cobalt #1E3A8A → #1A3275 in a 5% shift across the field.
  * Foil-gradient: brass #C9A24B → #B08937 → #C9A24B as a soft 3-stop reading like a     foil-stamp highlight catching light.
  * Velvet-gradient: burgundy #4A1621 falling into charred black #0B0B0B at one corner,     like low candlelight.

DROP-SHADOW RULE:
  Only add a drop shadow to type when the palette contrast is INSUFFICIENT on its own   (e.g., cream type over a busy mid-tone photo). When contrast is already strong   (e.g., brass-foil gold on deep forest green, cream on charred black), OMIT drop   shadows entirely — they cheapen the foil-stamp effect. Foil type on a deep tonal   field needs no shadow; the foil itself carries the hierarchy.

FINAL: Render at the aspect ratio specified via the API (not in prose). Dense typography with purpose. Real brand register. No stock gradients, no Canva-template feel. Avoid the AI tells: no cyan-to-magenta gradients, no default Poppins/Montserrat/Open Sans, no 33/33/33 colour balance, no centered dead-symmetric layouts unless ceremonial, no flat-colour fields. Every surface is LAYERED: photo + gradient + noise + emboss / foil / grain.
